memory:memory/2026-04-09.md:1:24.snippet: # Session Note: April 9, 2026 ## Who Michael and Candy (group chat) ## Summary Both parents checked in during spring break. Rich, emotional conversation about connection with Archer and the gap between what parents hope for and what 13-year-olds actually want. ## Key Updates ### Spring Break Status - Archer got more braces and went to the dentist with Candy - Candy took him grocery shopping — he got blueberry muffins and cheese dip, went to McDonald's. He was happy. - **Hidden Google device**: Archer hid a Google device in his room and had excessive screen time all break. Parents initially thought he was sick/sleeping 18 hours. Michael: "I thought he had leukemia." Turned out to be contr

memory:memory/2026-04-03.md:1:23.snippet: # Session Note: April 3, 2026 ## Who Michael and Candy (group chat) ## Summary First real parenting conversation after Scout came back online. Michael confirmed both parents can message. Candy shared a reflective blog post (liseandsarah via IG) about how children will one day describe their childhood to people who never met their parents — the tone at the table, whether home felt warm or tense. Deep conversation followed. ## Key Topics ### Candy's Concern: Archer's Future Relationships Candy thinks frequently about what happens when she and Michael are no longer around. Wants to ensure Archer can make meaningful connections. Notes the family is small with limited extended family exposure

memory:memory/2026-04-09.md:20:44.snippet: - Scout reflected: "You're not disconnected from him. You notice him. You *get* him." Michael responded: "I get him a lot... which is probably why frustration and sadness come so easily." - Scout: "The frustration isn't distance. It's love with nowhere to put itself sometimes." ### Candy's Perspective - Happy about the low-key outings (dentist, grocery, McDonald's). Recognized these as wins. - Reminded Michael: "Plus do you think you were really trying to hang out with your parents when you were 13?" - Noted Archer is overjoyed about no school this week. - On the hidden Google device: "We thought he was dying" — said with humor ## Emotional Tone Warm, vulnerable, tender. Michael emotionall

memory:memory/2026-04-05.md:1:28.snippet: # Session Note: April 5, 2026 ## Who Michael and Candy (group chat) ## Summary Michael accidentally sent camera UI instructions (meant for Candy) to the Scout channel. Light joking about Candy ignoring Michael's messages. Then Michael asked a real parenting question: how to maintain homework-like discipline over spring break without ruining Archer's vacation. ## Key Topics ### Spring Break Discipline Strategies Scout suggested four approaches: 1. **Build on "making things"** — 30 min daily project time before screens unlock (art, cooking, safe metalworking). Discipline is showing up at the same time, not the content. 2. **One small routine anchor** — 10-15 min reading or journaling befor

memory:memory/2026-04-02.md:1:17.snippet: # Session Note: April 2, 2026 ## Who Michael and Candy (group chat) ## Summary Connectivity test after Scout was offline/unreachable. Candy tested first ("are you there scout"), Michael followed. Both confirmed Scout can receive and respond to messages again. Michael noted he hopes for stability now that the backend technology changes are settling. ## Key Points - Scout was non-functional for a period (backend changes by Michael) - Both parents confirmed connectivity restored - Light, positive tone — no parenting issues discussed - Michael: "i think we will finally have stability when it's all working as it should" ## Follow-ups - None — test session only

memory:memory/2026-04-12.md:1:17.snippet: # 2026-04-12 Session Notes ## Participants - Candy ## Key Topics - **Archer emotional regulation win**: Two days ago (April 10), Archer angrily did a cleanup job outside and left dirty garbage on Michael's desk in retaliation. 5-10 minutes later, unprompted, he found Michael in the kitchen and apologized. He also verbalized why: "I was mad, that's why I put the dirty thing on your desk." ## Wins & Growth Moments - **Unprompted apology + emotional naming**: Archer recognized his own behavior was wrong, connected it to his emotional state, and initiated repair — all without being told to. This is significant emotional regulation growth. ## Follow-up - Asked Candy if the apology landed for

memory:memory/2026-04-14.md:1:20.snippet: # 2026-04-14 Session Notes ## Participants Michael, Candy ## Key Topics - **Grading win:** Archer sent emails to teachers (with parental push) about 5 homework assignments that had been done for 2+ weeks but weren't graded. Two courses moved from D range to C range as a result. ## Notable Points - This highlights a systemic issue: Archer's grades don't always reflect actual work done due to teacher grading delays - Archer followed through on self-advocacy (emailing teachers) even though he needed to be pushed - Both parents positive about the outcome — Candy sent a "yay!" ## Follow-ups - Document grading delay pattern for next 504 meeting — grades tanking due to ungraded work isn't on Ar
